children.value: The static publishing pipeline is built and live.

GitHub Pages serves from

Action for the human: open both URLs, check rendering and search,
temper if it holds up.
SPA routing on GitHub Pages needs

No server
rewrites available; the 404 page bootstraps the SPA. Standard
pattern, but worth recording — every future Pages publication will
want this.

(Pages-conventional) but rejected: collides with potential
per-repo README/doc folder;

during the work. Originally scoped
as “user wraps

in their own shell”; in practice the deploy
shape (git checkout, copy bundle, commit, push) was identical
across publications and earned a built-in verb. The “users can
still wrap it themselves” property is preserved —

None blocking. The shape is settled; review and temper decides
whether anything wants follow-up.
Build the static publishing surface for fibers.

, and
emits per-fiber JSON + a Fuse search index + a tiny

(or a user-built shell)
pushes the bundle to a GitHub Pages repo. Vellum-reader becomes
reader

as runtime deps;
React stays peer-dep-only.
: unchanged. No new verbs, no config.
defaults to 1 (single fiber, 0 means all descendants);

. In-bundle wikilinks
navigate internally; out-of-bundle render as stub cards. Bare-slug
collisions error at bake time.

Done means: bake + publish roundtrip works end-to-end, two live
publications serve and render, README screenshot in place,
vellum-reader installable via

. Explicitly out of
scope: npm-registry publish,

verb, asset offload
(R2/S3), cross-publication wikilink resolution, astra-rendering in
the static adapter, round-tripping giscus comments back into fibers.
This is the publishing surface piece of the broader

arc: vellum-reader was just extracted from the lightcone monorepo,
and it needs a way to publish fibers as static sites. The
“tapestries” repo is the demo target — it shows what a published
fiber surface looks like.
Pushing to GitHub Pages is the user’s shell (vellum-reader provides
, but any user can equally well wrap

in their own
deploy script). This keeps felt out of the publishing business
entirely: bake to a directory, let the user push. Felt itself stays
15 MB and zero-config; the producer/consumer symmetry lives in one
repo (vellum-reader).
The architectural pivot from earlier drafts — bake moved

of
felt and into vellum-reader — is recorded in

; the
short reason is that the original plan (felt-owned bake with

Go verbs shelling out to TS) changed felt’s character for
every user, most of whom never publish.

frontmatter.name: Tapestries: vellum-reader bake, GitHub Pages deploy
frontmatter.outcome: Pipeline landed; two publications live. Review the surface and temper if it holds up — see Current State.
